parameters.message: =✅ Capture demo requests with HubSpot and Microsoft Teams without manual  — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Lead Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Company, Score). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Stamp & Shape Lead

### Stamp & Shape Lead

Shapes the item: writes `leadId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.leadId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Route by Work Type · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: inbound, outbound + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'inbound' → HubSpot · ②→ Fast-Track 'outbound' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'inbound' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Fast-Track 'outbound' + Alert

Posts a message to Slack (`#sales`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty email

### Guard Non-Empty email

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

### Append Audit Row (HubSpot)

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Microsoft Teams · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Capture demo requests with HubSpot and Microsoft Teams without manual work

**Pattern:** Lead Capture & Routing · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Capture demo requests with HubSpot and Microsoft Teams without manual work. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual outreach is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Lead Form Submission → Stamp & Shape Lead → Payload Valid? → Route by Work Type → Process 'inbound' → HubSpot → Merge Routed Work → Guard Non-Empty email → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to lead capture & routing without manual effort
- Anyone automating outreach in sales
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: HubSpot private-app token; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your outreach process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
